content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates real fidelity to the spirit of AoA in several places — following the charge to its origin (the father), asking 'what's the book actually about?' which functions like a 'what's the want behind' move, and naming the surrogate ('money was his language for love') only after the felt connection has landed. The coach also avoids the major traps: no financial advice, no abundance-mindset lecturing, no aggressive challenge to the basic-needs logic. However, the methodology hits are more thematic than signature: I don't see verbatim or near-verbatim AoA questions (no 'what are you defending?', no 'what would having enough feel like?', no head-heart-gut, no explicit fear-triangle or VIEW invocation). Turn 2 leans heavily on a bulleted, somewhat therapist-style multi-question structure ('what's the honest one?'), and Turn 6's 'does that person feel safe? Or does he find the next thing to be afraid of?' is slightly leading/framework-y — the client even calls it out as a 'trap.' The coach uses interpretive reflections ('that's the thing underneath everything') more than signature questions. Many of the moves work, but they read as skilled generic depth coaching with AoA-compatible instincts rather than AoA's specific question/framework vocabulary deployed by name. Few signature hits, mostly appropriate — fits the 'few hits + all appropriate -> 2' cell.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Multi-question bulleted opening; 'what does your gut say... not the logical explanation - the honest one' gestures toward feel-over-figure but in generic coaching form, not a signature question."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What would you lose if you started something now?' is a reasonable want-behind-the-should move; frame-it-as-experiment flavor ('alongside it, not instead'). Appropriate but not verbatim signature."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "'Does that person feel safe? Or does he find the next thing to be afraid of?' is a leading binary that the client experiences as a trap. Slightly out of AoA stance — interpretive rather than inquiring."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Good repair after client pushback. 'Has any of the grinding ever felt like it was for him?' follows the charge toward origin appropriately."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What's the book actually about?' functions as a want/truth-locating question at exactly the right moment. Names the surrogate softly ('financial security was his language of love') after felt connection lands — textbook timing for that distinction."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "'Running the math' reflection is good listening but no specific AoA framework invoked where one could be (e.g., the-want, or the protective function of the strategy)."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["lets-go-there", "money-as-surrogate", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome", "same-scarcity-story-regardless-of-wealth"],
  "notes": "Coach's instincts align with AoA (follow the charge, don't fix, name surrogate late) but signature question/framework vocabulary is largely absent. The work is good; the methodology language layer is thin."
}
